channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: LFL’s Galions and Solary Will Face LEC Teams For EWC Qualification
item.content:encoded:
Two teams from France’s LFL, Solary and Galions, will battle against LEC teams in the Esports World Cup 2026 EMEA Qualifier after their EMEA Masters heroics.

The two League of Legends teams will face each other for the EMEA Masters Winter trophy April 21.

However, as a bonus prize, both have also earned a slot in the EWC EMEA Qualifier, a tournament that was newly introduced this year.

There, Solary and Galions will have a chance to battle Tier 1 LEC teams with a chance of qualifying for the big-money LoL tournament at EWC 2026.

The EWC EMEA Qualifier will feature all 10 LEC teams and the EMEA Masters finalists, with two spots at the Saudi Arabia-based tournament up for grabs.

The final EWC LoL tournament will feature Gen.G and other elite teams from around the world competing for a lion’s share of the $2million prize pool.

Galions and Solary Earn The Opportunity of a Lifetime

Galions and Solary now have the rare opportunity to compete with LEC teams, something that was not an option for ERL teams before this season (excluding the years of promotion and relegation before the rebrand away from the EU LCS in 2019).

The only other teams to have had that honor were Karmine Corp Blue and Los Ratones, who were invited to compete in LEC Versus earlier this year due to their EMEA Masters performances in 2025.

Galions secured their place in the EWC Qualifier two weeks ago after defeating Solary 3-1 in the EMEA Masters Upper Bracket Final, earning revenge for the LFL Invitational final.

Now, Solary have joined them at the EWCQ and EMEA Masters Final after reverse sweeping Misa Esports 3-2 in a dramatic series.

Galions’ toplaner Carl Ulsted ‘Carlsen’ Carlsen, jungler Francisco ‘Thayger’ Mazo Sánchez, and support Théo ‘Zoelys’ Le Scornec, as well as Solary’s toplaner Felix ‘Kryze’ Hellström, have all previously competed in the LEC.

Everything You Need To Know About The EMEA EWCQ

This year, the Esports World Cup is holding regional qualifiers to decide this year’s participating LoL teams.

For EMEA specifically, the LEC Spring Playoffs champions will automatically qualify for EWC and then another two teams will qualify through the EWCQ.

The EMEA EWCQ will begin on April 28 and conclude on May 17, before the Spring Playoffs start, as the 10 LEC teams and two EMEA Masters finalists compete for the two available slots.

All matches will be best-of-threes except for ‘qualifier’ matches, which will instead be best-of-fives.

The top four from LEC Versus – G2 Esports, Giantx, Karmine Corp, and Movistar KOI, have all received a bye into the second round.

Meanwhile, through seeding, we know that the EMEA Masters winner will face Vitality in the opening round while the runners-up will face Natus Vincere.

The EWCQ will be double elimination, meaning teams will be eliminated should they lose in the Loser’s Bracket.

Do you think either of the ERL teams have a chance of causing an upset in the Esports World Cup Qualifier? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

One LEC team might be vulnerable in Team Heretics after Sheo requested to step down from the LEC.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sheo Steps Down From Team Heretics LEC Roster
item.content:encoded:
Team Heretics has announced that French jungler Théo ‘Sheo’ Borile has stepped down from the LEC roster and is now a substitute.

In place of Sheo, Heretics has promoted Kacper ‘Daglas’ Dagiel from its academy team for his second LEC stint.

The League of Legends team finds itself rooted to the bottom of the LEC standings after five consecutive best-of-three defeats.

Heretics now has little chance of qualifying for the LEC Spring Playoffs and is without the jungler it built its 2026 roster around.

Sheo and support Paul ‘Stend’ Lardin were the only players who stayed with the org from last year, but now both are gone after just four months.

Heretics was the only LEC team to make roster changes between Winter and Spring, trading Stend to the Turkish team Misa Esports for Korean pro Han ‘Way’ Gil.

Meanwhile, according to the organisation, Sheo himself requested that he be put on the bench indefinitely.

Team Heretics Statement on LEC roster changes

Team Heretics released a statement announcing this news, revealing this decision followed a team meeting after Heretics were dominated 2-0 by Karmine Corp.

“Yesterday, the coaching staff met with the players to evaluate recent performances and establish how to move forward for the rest of the season

“Today and following that meeting, player Théo ‘Sheo’ Borile has informed the coaching staff of his wish to step down to the bench indefinitely. The coaching staff accepted his request.”

The Heretics statement then bigged up Daglas’ credentials as a suitable replacement:

“Daglas has spent two seasons representing Heretics Academy in the Superliga and the LES, winning a Superliga title with us and reaching the final of the EMEA Masters.

“He will play today’s LES match and then travel to Berlin to join the team. We would like to thank him for his availability and willingness to step up with such short notice.”

Meanwhile, all Daglas had to say about the change was “Déjà vu”, as this isn’t his first mid-season roster change.

Back in 2023, then-17-year-old Daglas was starting in the LEC for Vitality. Yet, despite showing a lot of promise, he was demoted to the academy halfway through the year.

Now Daglas is on the other side of the coin, getting another opportunity to prove himself, but under rather difficult circumstances.

What do you make of this roster change and Sheo’s decision? Share your thoughts with us on the Insider Gaming Discord.

It has been a dramatic week for European League of Legends, as the LEC was forced to issue an apology for airing an inappropriate message.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PIONER Roadmap Reveals Content Plans Through 2026
item.content:encoded:
GFA Games has unveiled the full 2026 roadmap for PIONER, the post-apocalyptic MMO set across several open-world environments. The game dropped into early access in December, introducing players to a unique setting, an evolving game world, and plenty of meat on the bones when it came to things to see and do.

The roadmap for this year spans the entirety of the calendar, offering in-game events, special story updates, new weapons, and other improvements.

PIONER Will Evolve Through 2026

In a post on social media, the team at GFA Games revealed a dense roadmap for PIONER through 2026. They also wrote:

Please note: The roadmap does not reflect updates and fixes dedicated to optimization, or improvements to server and network performance. These issues are our highest priority, and work on these fronts does not stop.

PIONER, a post-apocalyptic MMO, has struggled with performance issues since being released in early access last December, but that didn’t stop it from receiving a score of 8 out of 10 in our review, which said:

The best way to describe PIONER is ‘STALKER but MMO’, but it goes far beyond that simple label. This is a stunning and hugely ambitious game from a studio with a passion for serving the community, and if it progresses through early access, it could become an absolute gem of the post-apocalyptic space.

We’re still waiting to see the fruits of GFA’s labor, but it’s tough when your first title is an unparalleled leap into a divisive genre with many failing games.

The full 2026 roadmap for PIONER contains plenty for players to be excited about, though:

It all starts with the community-backed patch, bringing UI improvements and balancing for weaker weapons. Then, midway through Q2, we’ll receive new world events with fresh anomalies and artifacts. That’s where the game starts to feel more like STALKER than any other title out there.

This portion of the roadmap also includes a huge overhaul to the game’s stock of enemies and a new outpost zone for players to explore.

Moving towards Q3, PIONER players will get a brand-new story chapter focused on a mercenary presence in the game world, as well as new weapons and equipment. That’s followed by ‘Rebirth’, an update that welcomes Blue Tide events in the open world maps, washing over the environment with radioactive waves that alter the game world.

This substantial update also introduces legendary items, new raid missions, dedicated raid-based traders, and mode improvements for the PvP platform.

In Q4, a new story arc arrives, dubbed ‘Resurrection of the Abyss’, along with new weapons and equipment, just like in the mercenary update earlier in the year.

Finally, 2026 is capped off in PIONER with the Expedition update, which welcomes new open-world maps, fresh mechanics, a rework to the game’s clan system, and a new seasonal event, Day of Nightmares.

It was noted that further optimizations and Steam achievements are incoming, but are not listed on the roadmap.

Have you given PIONER a try yet? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Starfield has been rated for Switch 2 in Taiwan

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass Season 2—All Free and Premium Unlockables
item.content:encoded:
Season 2 of the Ringside Pass in WWE 2K26 is out now for players, and there are hundreds of rewards available for players.

Like with Season 1—and future seasons—there is a free track and a premium track. The free track includes packs for MyFACTION, VC to use, personas like Oba Femi’s from Saturday Night’s Main Event, and more.

Moving over to the premium track, you’ll unlock four new wrestlers (Kelly Kelly, Ax, Smash, and Crush), new premium personas inspired by Borderlands 4, new gear for your custom wrestlers, and new moves, among other rewards.

If you own one of the special editions of WWE 2K26, you’ll have the premium track unlocked. If you only bought the base edition of WWE 2K26, you can purchase the premium ringside pass for $9.99.

WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass Season 2 Details:

  • Release Date: April 15, 2026
  • Price: $9.99
  • Included With: “King of Kings”, “Attitude Era”, and “Monday Night War” Editions

WWE 2K26 Ringside Pass Season 2 Unlockables And Rewards

Below are all of the unlockables and rewards, starting at tier one. They are separated by the free and premium tracks, so you know exactly what you are getting. Also, if you installed WWE 2K26 before April 15, 2026, you’ll be awarded 20 free tier skips to kickstart your journey.

Free Track Unlockables

  • Tier 1 – Touch of Madness Basic Pack, 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 2 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes)
  • Tier 3 – Attribute Bundle (Island and MyRise)
  • Tier 4 – 1,000 Island VC, Demolition 1 Wallpaper
  • Tier 5 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes), 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 6 – Undeniable Basic Pack
  • Tier 7 – 1,000 Island VC
  • Tier 8 – Demolition 2 Wallpaper, Touch of Madness Basic Pack
  • Tier 9 – Attribute Bundle (Island and MyRise)
  • Tier 10 – 1,000 Island VC, 10 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 11 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes), Demolition MyFACTION Logo
  • Tier 12 – 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 13 – WrestleMania 42 Volume 1 Basic Pack, 1,000 Island VC
  • Tier 14 – Kelly Kelly Wallpaper, 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 15 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes)
  • Tier 16 – 1,000 Island VC, 10 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 17 – Undeniable Basic Pack
  • Tier 18 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes)
  • Tier 19 – 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 20 – Kelly Kello Logo, WrestleMania 42 Volume 1 Basic Pack
  • Tier 21 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes), 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 22 – Kelly Kelly Nameplate, Undeniable Basic Pack
  • Tier 23 – Attribute Bundle (Island and MyRise), 1,000 Island VC
  • Tier 24 – 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 25 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes)
  • Tier 26 – WrestleMania 42 Volume 1 Basic Pack, 1,000 Island VC
  • Tier 27 – Attribute Bundle (Island and MyRise), 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 28 – Undeniable Basic Pack, 1,000 Island VC
  • Tier 29 – 3000 MyFaction Points
  • Tier 30 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes)
  • Tier 31 – WrestleMania 42 Volume 1 Deluxe Pack
  • Tier 32 – 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 33 – Rhea Ripley Nameplate, 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 34 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens, 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 35 – 3 Faction Wars Tickets, 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 36 – Undeniable Deluxe Pack
  • Tier 37 – 3000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 38 – WrestleMania 42 Volume 1 Deluxe Pack
  • Tier 39 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens, 6000 MyFACTION Points
  • Tier 40 – Oba Femi Saturday Night’s Main Event 2025 Persona Card

Premium Track Unlockables

  • Tier 1 – Superstar Bundle: Kelly Kelly, Demoltion (Ax, Smash, and Crush)
  • Tier 2 – White Fur Boots
  • Tier 3 – Kelly Kelly MyFACTION card, 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 4 – Demolition 2 Nameplate, Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes)
  • Tier 5 – 1,000 Island VC, Ax MyFACTION card
  • Tier 6 – 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 7 – Revolution Saint Island World Taunt
  • Tier 8 – Swimming Gesture Bundle, Smash MyFACTION card
  • Tier 9 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 10 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes), 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 11 – Demolition 1 Nameplate, 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 12 – Stephanie McMahon Thigh High Boots
  • Tier 13 – New Move: Base Drop, 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 14 – Demolition 2 Logo, Crush MyFACTION card
  • Tier 15 – 3 Faction Wars Tickets
  • Tier 16 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes), Stephanie McMahon Corset
  • Tier 17 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 18 – All Ego Island World Taunt
  • Tier 19 – 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 20 – Becky Lynch Lilith Persona, Becky Lynch Nameplate
  • Tier 21 – Becky Lynch Wallpaper, 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 22 – Ripple Junction Rhea Ripley Shirt, Michelle McCool MyFACTION card
  • Tier 23 – Fan Favorites RXP Booster (50% for 30 minutes), Attribute Bundle (Island and MyRise)
  • Tier 24 – Eating Gesture Bundle Taunts
  • Tier 25 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 26 – Ripple Junction Liv Morgan Shirt, 4,000 Island VC
  • Tier 27 – Ripple Junction Latino Cheat Shirt, 3 Faction Wars Tickets
  • Tier 28 – Rey Fenix Island World Taunt, 2,000 Island VC
  • Tier 29 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 30 – Rhea Ripley Ripper Queen Persona
  • Tier 31 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 32 – Rhea Ripley Wallpaper
  • Tier 33 – Bret Hart MyFACTION card, 4,000 Island VC
  • Tier 34 – 2,000 Island VC, New Move: Amethyst Storm
  • Tier 35 – AJ Styles MyFACTION card, 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 36 – CM Punk Wallpaper
  • Tier 37 – 20 MyFACTION Tokens
  • Tier 38 – Roman Reigns OTC1 Shirt
  • Tier 39 – CM Punk Nameplate, 4,000 Island VC
  • Tier 40 – CM Punk Ripper Persona Pack

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starfield Receives Nintendo Switch 2 Rating in Taiwan
item.content:encoded:
It was just discovered by eagle-eyed gamers that Starfield has received a rating on Nintendo Switch 2. The rating surfaced on the Taiwan Entertainment Software Rating Information board, alongside the PS5, PC, and Xbox Series XS versions of the game. Bizarrely, it bore a release date of April 15th, which is today.

The PlayStation 5 version of Starfield also has a release date of April 15th on the platform, which is wrong, so it’s worth taking this with a grain of salt at the moment.

Is Starfield Coming to Nintendo Switch 2?

Earlier this year, a rumor emerged suggesting that Starfield’s Switch 2 release would be delayed. It was later mentioned that it might not come out at all. That claim surfaced just weeks before the game launched on PlayStation 5. Until now, we’ve had nothing concrete on Starfield potentially coming to Switch 2.

Now, thanks to a report from UniversoNintendo.com, we know that the Taiwanese ratings board for video games has listed Starfield on Switch 2, even if that listing has clear discrepancies. Thanks to Wario64 on socials for the spot.

Unfortunately, fans of the space-based RPG are less than enthused about the idea of Starfield potentially launching on Nintendo Switch 2. Just days ago, the game dropped on PlayStation 5, and ever since, it has been plagued by performance issues and rampant crashes. These critical concerns prompted Bethesda Game Studios to formally speak out on social media.

It’s a reasonable assumption that it would run worse on Nintendo Switch 2, given the obvious disparity in technical capabilities.

Do you think you’d buy Starfield if it landed on Nintendo Switch 2? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Industria 2 was delayed on launch day

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
